items.title: Teen dies after police find him inside vehicle with 'serious stab wound'

children.content: A 19-year-old man has died and another has been airlifted to hospital with serious injuries after a stabbing incident in the Northern Territory town of Katherine overnight.

items.title: Darwin records second Indigenous death in custody within a week

children.content: In less than a week, another Indigenous man in his 20s has died in custody in Darwin. The incidents were not related, but the cause of death for both men remains unknown.

image.altText: An aerial photo of a correctional facility.

items.title: Convenience stores selling illegal tobacco in Darwin spark organised crime fears

children.content: Convenience stores selling cheap, illegally packaged cigarettes have been popping up across Darwin and businesses are worried that organised crime and gang violence are next.

image.altText: Convenience store with red and black signage

items.title: St John NT warns 'extraordinary' weekend measures 'absolutely not' safe

children.content: Northern Territory paramedics pushed "well and truly beyond our limits" at the weekend say the "extraordinary" measures taken in response are unsustainable and "absolutely not" safe.

articles.title: Darwin's ambulance service fails overnight as 61 Triple Zero calls abandoned

children.content: Darwin's ambulance service has reached a "critical failure point" as 42 per cent of Triple Zero calls were abandoned overnight.

image.altText: Triple Zero emergency sign on St John NT ambulance

gallery.altText: Female paramedic looks ahead with serious face

thumbnail.altText: Female paramedic looks ahead with serious face

items.title: Outback tourist town up in arms over proposed policing plan

children.content: When residents in Pine Creek call police in an emergency, the nearest 24-hour station is 100 kilometres away.

image.altText: A white brick wall with balck lettering that reads pine creek police station with a coat of arms above it

items.title: Telstra outage in remote NT community impacting Triple Zero access

children.content: Residents in the remote NT community of Kintore have had difficulty making Triple Zero calls during a six-day Telstra outage, which has also caused issues buying fuel.

image.altText: An aerial view of a town

items.title: Fish prices to rise as fuel costs escalate, commercial industry warns

children.content: Farmers, fishers and business leaders are appealing to the federal and state governments to provide targeted relief to help them deal with massive fuel price increases caused by the Iran War, and the looming prospect of shortages.

gallery.altText: Two men in light coloured caps, one in a checked shirt, the other in a blue shirt stand in front of a bed of colourful flowers

thumbnail.altText: Two men in light coloured caps, one in a checked shirt, the other in a blue shirt stand in front of a bed of colourful flowers

items.title: Trump wants to 'take the oil' in Iran, as PM urges service stations to 'do the right thing'

children.content: US President Donald Trump says he wanted to "take the oil" in Iran. Follow our live coverage.

image.altText: A close up of Donald Trump.

items.title: ASX down 0.8pc to follow Wall Street, oil up sharply as Houthis enter war

children.content: The ASX 200 has recovered some of its earlier losses to be down by just 0.8 per cent in Monday trade after investors dumped banks and tech stocks in the wake of Wall Street losses on Friday night. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Man points to ASX board, in red and black colours, in Sydney.

items.title: Trump has 'no problem' with Russian oil shipment to Cuba

children.content: President Donald Trump has said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a US oil blockade.

image.altText: A man sits around a collection of jerry cans as another man lifts a clear container of water, moving to put it in a bag.

items.title: Storm forward hospitalised after suspected stroke in 'medical episode'

children.content: The Melbourne Storm say Tui Kamikamica is receiving the best possible care after being rushed to a hospital following a "medical episode" this morning.

image.altText: Tui Kamikamica carries the ball

items.title: As universities cut courses, spending on consultants and contractors nears $2b

children.content: Consultancies have been accused of infiltrating universities and wasting scarce public funds on questionable advice about cutting courses and jobs.

gallery.altText: A student walks by several others sitting in a grassy area. Behind them is a modern glass building.
Several buildings can be seen with UTS signage. One is a concrete brutalist tower. Another is modern steel.

thumbnail.altText: A student walks by several others sitting in a grassy area. Behind them is a modern glass building.
Several buildings can be seen with UTS signage. One is a concrete brutalist tower. Another is modern steel.

items.title: Secret tunnels used in plot to smuggle one tonne of cocaine into Australia, police say

children.content: Australian police have charged six crew members of the high-profile MV Raider vessel with drug smuggling, saying they attempted to bring 1 tonne of cocaine into Australia after being intercepted by French Polynesian authorities earlier this year.

image.altText: A police officer looking through a tunnel

items.title: Coroner calls for stricter gun laws after father-son murder-suicide

children.content: An inquest into a father-son murder-suicide has led the NSW Coroner to recommend that doctors be legally required to alert police if they have concerns about patients who own guns.

image.altText: man and boy

items.title: Rare durian variety sells for $200 at auction

children.content: A rare fruit has snared top dollar at auction at a Far North Queensland festival over the weekend.

image.altText: Composite image of small durian fruit, and huge fruit display.

items.title: Aussies cruise to ODI series win over West Indies with dominant second game

children.content: Australia takes an unassailable 2-0 lead over the West Indies with a cruisy 91-run victory in the second ODI at St Kitts' Warner Park.

image.altText: Beth Mooney hits out

items.title: How to discuss additional work-from-home days with your employer

children.content: With rising fuel prices adding additional financial stress to household living costs, what are your rights when it comes to working from home — and what are the best ways to approach your employer about flexible work arrangements?

gallery.altText: A woman sits in front of a computer monitor at home, with her cat sitting on a windowsill

thumbnail.altText: A woman sits in front of a computer monitor at home, with her cat sitting on a windowsill

avatar.alt: Portrait of journalist Daniel Johnson wearing a grey and maroon plaid shirt and crossing his arms.

items.title: Photos explore psychological drama of Australian rural life

children.content: Western Australian documentary photographer Brad Rimmer's beautiful, melancholy images of Wheatbelt life sprawl through Fremantle gallery.

image.altText: Darkened corridor with series of black and white images overlaid with deep colour

items.title: The best and worst things about being on reality TV, according to Guy Sebastian

children.content: Singing his way into the Australian public's hearts way back in 2003, Guy Sebastian has since had a fascinating and varied career. We catch up with the star to find out about the little things that keep him going — from his two sons to his favourite TV show.

image.altText: Guy Sebastian sits behind a table smiling. Behind him are shop mannequins and sale signs

items.title: Exmouth beaches a 'graveyard' after ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle

children.content: In the popular tourist destination, it appears the category four cyclone has chewed up and spat out whole sections of the Ningaloo Reef onto dry land.

articles.title: Driving south out of Exmouth not possible in 'forseeable future'

children.content: Visitors in Exmouth who were stuck there when Cyclone Narelle passed are now trying to find ways out of the town.

image.altText: A cardboard on a cafe bench with handwriting on it

items.title: SA rules out free public transport, for now, as dozens of servos are out of some fuels

children.content: The South Australian government has no plans to follow other states in offering free public transport despite more than 70 service stations running out of at least one fuel type.

image.altText: A tram at a level crossing with a bus waiting behind boomgates

items.title: Council meeting descends into disarray as councillors' faces obscured

children.content: The incident sparked a debate over conspiracies, technology, TikTok, and clout-chasing in Australia's largest local council.

image.altText: A blurry photo taken from a webcam in a virtual meeting

items.title: One Nation claims seat of MacKillop, its third in South Australia's lower house

children.content: A former immigration detention system worker on Christmas Island has won the seat of MacKillop.

image.altText: A headshot of a man smiling at the camera.

items.title: 36ers lodge complaint to NBL over Bogut's 'personal attacks' on owner

children.content: Drama from the spiteful championship series between the Adelaide 36ers and Sydney Kings goes off the court as the 36ers complain to the NBL about the conduct of Kings co-owner and assistant Andrew Bogut.

image.altText: Andrew Bogut points with his left hand

items.title: Commuters say buses 'absolutely packed' as free transport starts

children.content: Some commuters in Hobart say the buses have been busier than usual, as free public transport rolls out across Tasmania.

image.altText: People walk off a Metro Tas bus in Hobart's CBD.

items.title: Decisions made more than a decade ago are coming home to roost

children.content: The early 2010s were the years of peak globalisation, when it seemed all trade barriers were on a one-way trip to elimination. That was before Donald Trump arrived and ruptured the global order.

image.altText: Towers at an oil refinery.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Rising fuel costs force Australians to rethink Easter holidays

children.content: Rising fuel prices driven by the Middle East conflict are hitting the domestic tourism sector, with cancellations surging ahead of the Easter long weekend.

articles.title: Next six months to be 'horrific': Food charities hit by twin crises

children.content: Charities supplying food relief to struggling Australians are grappling with the twin pressures of rising demand for help while their own fuel bills soar.

image.altText: A woman in a navy cap and white shirt stands beside the open door of a red car holding a silver box.

articles.title: Rural families consider giving up sport and school runs as fuel prices soar

children.content: Rural and remote families who are spending as much as $500 a week on fuel amid the current oil crisis say they are preparing to make tough decisions about whether to make school runs and to go without sport and even health care.

image.altText: Two primary school children outside their rural school.

items.title: South Australia wins back-to-back Shield titles with stunning win over Victoria

children.content: South Australia has won back-to-back Sheffield Shield titles with a brilliant comeback victory over Victoria in the final at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

image.altText: South Australia bowler Henry Thornton jumps, shouts and punches the air to celebrate a Sheffield Shield wicket.

items.title: 'I feel betrayed': New scam figures show Australians lost more than $2 billion

children.content: Australians lost more than $2 billion to scammers in 2025, the latest data from the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has revealed.

image.altText: A man wearing a hoodie holds a laptop

items.title: Nepal's former PM arrested over deaths during protests

children.content: Nepalese police arrests former prime minister Khadga Prasad Oli as they investigate whether he was negligent in failing to prevent dozens of deaths during a crackdown on anti-corruption protests last September.

image.altText: Nepal Prime Minister Khadga Prasad Oli being escorted by police in uniform

items.title: King penguin's arrival in WA prompts 'high-level' biosecurity response

children.content: The king penguin, known for being the second-largest penguin species and living on ice-free parts of the sub-Antarctic, was rescued near Esperance on WA's south coast.

image.altText: woman in blue polo and hat leaning by a penguin, she is reflected in a mirror behind her.

items.title: Teen says single parent bias is leaving Australian families homeless

children.content: Hunter was plunged into homelessness when his family lost their rental. It is an increasingly similar story to other single parents finding themselves "lifelong renters".

gallery.altText: A young man smiles in front of branches.
